title: USS George H.W. Bush CSG Completes Transit to 5th Fleet, Three US Carriers Now in CENTCOM AOR
summary: The George H.W. Bush Carrier Strike Group completed its transit to the US 5th Fleet area, joining Abraham Lincoln and Gerald R. Ford CSGs for the first triple-carrier Middle East deployment since 2003.
contents:
## Fleet Tracking Update: Triple Carrier Deployment Confirmed

### Current US Carrier Positions (May 2, 2026)
- **CVN-77 USS George H.W. Bush**: Arabian Sea/Indian Ocean — recently arrived in 5th Fleet AOR
- **CVN-72 USS Abraham Lincoln**: Arabian Sea — supporting blockade operations outside Gulf of Oman
- **CVN-78 USS Gerald R. Ford**: Northern Red Sea — on station
- **CVN-71 USS Theodore Roosevelt**: US 3rd Fleet AOR — workup phase, integrating MDUSV Seahawk
- **CVN-73 USS George Washington**: In port, Yokosuka, Japan (forward-deployed)
- **CVN-68 USS Nimitz**: Off Chile coast — Southern Seas 2026 exercise

### Escort Forces in CENTCOM
Nine guided-missile destroyers accompany the three deployed CSGs, carrying a combined 846 VLS tubes. The Tripoli ARG (LHA-7) with USS New Orleans (LPD-18) and elements of the 31st MEU also operates in the CENTCOM area.

### Maintenance Status
USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (CVN-69) completed sea trials on April 24 following a 15-month maintenance availability. USS Carl Vinson (CVN-70) continues scheduled maintenance. Four of America's eleven carriers remain in maintenance or scheduled availability.

### Strategic Significance
This concentration of naval power matches the largest US carrier deployment to the region since Operation Iraqi Freedom in 2003. The positioning supports the ongoing blockade of Iranian oil exports and provides rapid strike capability should the ceasefire collapse.
